title: 1) exp1 — Linux system calls de
steps:
nano exp1.c

Paste the code and save.

Create the input file:

echo "Hello from test file" > test.txt

Compile:

gcc exp1.c -o exp1

Run:

./exp1

title: exp2 — Implement cp using Kernel APIs
steps:
Step 1: Create file
nano exp2.c
(Paste the code → Save with CTRL + X, then Y, then Enter)

Step 2: Compile
gcc exp2.c -o exp2

Step 3: Create a sample file
echo "Hello OS Lab" > file1.txt

Step 4: Run program
./exp2 file1.txt file2.txt

Step 5: Verify
cat file2.txt

Output:
Hello OS Lab
filename: exp2.pdf — Implement cp using Kernel APIs.txt

exp11 — Distributed System Samba
code:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install samba -y
mkdir ~/shared_folder
chmod -R 777 ~/shared_folder
sudo nano /etc/samba/smb.conf

[SharedFolder]
path = /home/user/shared_folder
brouseable = yes
writable = yes
guest ok = yes

sudo systemctl restart smbd
sudo systemctl status smbd
sudo apt install smbclient -y
smbclient //localhost/SharedFolder
echo "hello" > test.txt
put test.txt
ls ~/shared_folder
steps:
1. Update system:
   sudo apt update

2. Install Samba:
   sudo apt install samba -y

3. Create shared folder:
   mkdir ~/shared_folder

4. Set permissions:
   chmod -R 777 ~/shared_folder

5. Configure Samba:
   sudo nano /etc/samba/smb.conf

Add:
[SharedFolder]
path = /home/user/shared_folder
brouseable = yes
writable = yes
guest ok = yes

6. Restart service:
   sudo systemctl restart smbd

7. Check status:
   sudo systemctl status smbd

8. Install client:
   sudo apt install smbclient -y

9. Connect:
   smbclient //localhost/SharedFolder

10. Create file:
    echo "hello" > test.txt

11. Upload:
    put test.txt

12. Verify:
    ls ~/shared_folder
